entry.title: Institutions encode information—not only incentives—and reform should be evaluated on epistemic grounds as well as efficiency.
entry.summary: Same core evidence; v2 adds explicit scope boundary and refines the adversarial response after new counterexamples were reviewed.

entry.title: For this firm, methodological discipline is itself the product: public standing should track demonstrated process, not episodic visibility.
entry.summary: Internal scoreboard + publication policy commitments reduce overconfidence incentives; adversarial engagement is treated as a gate, not theater.

entry.title: Institutions encode information—not only incentives—and reform should be evaluated on epistemic grounds as well as efficiency.
entry.summary: Cross-episode claim chains link incentive stories to error-correction structures; adversarial challenges on reductionism were engaged and answered with explicit mechanisms.
